compiled_state.title: [review workflow]: orchestrator dispatches generic sub-agent for light code review -> named dso:code-reviewer-light agent not used
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** User-Flagged Behavior

#### Observed Behavior

During a light-tier code review dispatch (REVIEW_TIER=light), the orchestrator used `subagent_type: general-purpose` with model haiku instead of dispatching the named `dso:code-reviewer-light` agent. The REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 4 requires dispatching named `dso:code-reviewer-*` agents — dispatching a generic agent with review instructions is fabrication per CLAUDE.md rule #15. The incorrect output from the generic agent (a false-positive critical finding) confirmed the behavioral gap: the named agent's checklist and scoring rules were not applied.

#### Expected Behavior

For light-tier reviews, the orchestrator should load the code-reviewer-light.md agent file verbatim and dispatch it with subagent_type: general-purpose + model: haiku — passing the agent file content as the prompt. The named agent's output contract, scoring rules, and tier-specific checklist must govern the review.

#### Root Cause (Hypothesis)

The review workflow instruction 'inline dispatch is mandatory — dso:* labels are agent file identifiers, NOT subagent_type values' was not followed. The orchestrator constructed its own prompt instead of loading and passing the named agent file verbatim.

comments.body: Triaged (debug-everything Bug-Fix Mode): bug targets an LLM-behavioral skill/agent/prompt file. Per CLAUDE.md rule 22 and behavioral-testing-standard Rule 5, any modification requires dedicated bot-psychologist investigation and explicit user approval. DEFERRED: batch-escalating to end-of-session user review; no code change applied this session.
comments.author: Test
